title: rosa communis var. hispidula
intro: Rosa communis var. hispidula, also known as the wild rose or hip rose, is a perennial plant species belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of vitamin C and antioxidants, making it beneficial for immune system support and inflammation reduction. The hips of the Rosa communis var. hispidula plant are often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and jams, to promote overall health and wellness, including digestive health and anti-aging properties. Traditional herbal preparations of Rosa communis var. hispidula include drying the hips to make a powder or syrup, or infusing them in hot water to create a soothing tea.
